module.title: Emergency Fund Building
module.description: Learn how to build a financial safety net to protect yourself from unexpected expenses and financial emergencies.
learning_objectives: Understand what an emergency fund is and why it matters
Calculate how much you should save for emergencies
Learn where to safely store emergency savings
Avoid common mistakes when building an emergency fund

lessons.title: What Is an Emergency Fund?
lessons.content: An emergency fund is money set aside to cover unexpected expenses such as medical bills, car repairs, or job loss. It prevents you from relying on credit cards or loans during financial emergencies.

lessons.title: How Much Should You Save?
lessons.content: Most experts recommend saving 3 to 6 months of essential living expenses. Your target depends on job stability, income consistency, and personal risk level.

lessons.title: How to Build Your Emergency Fund
lessons.content: Start small and stay consistent. Even saving $20–$50 per week can build a strong emergency fund over time. Automating savings helps ensure consistency.

lessons.title: Where to Keep Your Emergency Fund
lessons.content: Your emergency fund should be stored in a safe and accessible place like a high-yield savings account. It should NOT be invested in risky assets like stocks.

lessons.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
lessons.content: Common mistakes include investing emergency funds in the stock market, not having a clear target, and using the fund for non-emergencies.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of an emergency fund?
questions.explanation: An emergency fund is meant to cover unexpected financial emergencies.

questions.question: Where should an emergency fund typically be stored?
questions.explanation: A high-yield savings account keeps money safe and easily accessible.

questions.question: How many months of expenses is commonly recommended?
questions.explanation: Experts typically recommend 3–6 months of essential expenses.
